prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f0100645`.
codex_response:
Implemented `f0100645` as a new ARC2 task package. The rule is: treat the non-border cells in the left-edge color and right-edge color as diagonal-connected blobs, then slide each left-color blob left and each right-color blob right until blocked by the matching border or by an already-settled blob on that side. I used the `opus46` hint as the starting hypothesis and discarded the conflicting `sonnet45` hint because it incorrectly described a row-count collapse instead of blob-preserving motion; that mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f0100645/notes.md).

Validation passed end to end: official verification was `3/3`, generated verification was `1000/1000`, the build produced the required artifacts, and the preview sheet looked on-distribution. No new primitive in `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was needed.
